prompts.label: 🔥 Prompt 1 — Copy & Paste into Claude
prompts.title: Complete React Frontend with Cart, Checkout & Razorpay
prompts.text:
Build me a complete, production-ready React + TypeScript ecommerce frontend using Vite. This is for a local Indian business (kiryana/general store) that wants a quick-commerce-style online ordering experience at zero cost.

=== BUSINESS DETAILS (REPLACE WITH YOUR OWN) ===
- Store Name: [Your Store Name — e.g., "Sharma General Store"]
- Store Tagline: [One line — e.g., "Ghar baithe grocery mangao"]
- Store Location: [Your area — e.g., "Varanasi, UP"]
- Store Phone: [WhatsApp number — e.g., "+91 98765 43210"]
- Product Categories: [e.g., "Grocery, Dairy, Snacks, Beverages, Household, Spices, Personal Care"]
- Currency: INR (₹)
- Delivery Charge: ₹40 flat for delivery, free for self-pickup

=== PRODUCTS ===
Add 15-20 products. For each product provide:
- Name, Price (₹), Category, Short description, Emoji (as image placeholder)

Example products:
1. Aashirvaad Atta 5kg | ₹280 | Grocery | "Premium whole wheat flour" | 🍞
2. Amul Milk 1L | ₹65 | Dairy | "Full cream pasteurized milk" | 🥛
3. Maggi Noodles Pack | ₹56 | Snacks | "2-minute masala noodles (4-pack)" | 🍜
4. Tata Salt 1kg | ₹28 | Grocery | "Iodized vacuum evaporated salt" | 🧂
[Add your own products here...]

=== PAGE STRUCTURE ===

PAGE 1 — PRODUCT CATALOG (Home Page "/")
- Header with store name, tagline, and language toggle (Hindi/English)
- Category filter pills — horizontal scrollable on mobile, clickable to filter products
- Product grid: 2 columns on mobile, 3-4 on desktop
- Each product card shows: emoji, name, price, category badge, "Add to Cart" button
- Quantity stepper appears after adding (+ / - buttons with count)
- Floating cart button (bottom-right) showing total items count as a badge
- Search bar to filter products by name

PAGE 2 — PRODUCT DETAIL ("/product/:id")
- Full product info: emoji, name, price, description, category
- Add to cart with quantity stepper
- "Related Products" section showing 4 products from same category

PAGE 3 — CHECKOUT ("/checkout")
- Order summary: all cart items with name, qty, price, subtotal per item
- Delivery type toggle: 🚚 Delivery (+₹40) vs 🏪 Self Pickup (FREE)
- Customer form fields:
  * Name (required)
  * Email (required) — for order confirmation and payment link
  * Phone (optional) — for WhatsApp updates
  * Delivery notes (optional) — "Gate no. 3, blue building" etc.
- Grand total calculation: subtotal + delivery charge (live updating)
- "Place Order" button — submits order to backend API (POST /api/order)

PAGE 4 — OTP VERIFICATION ("/verify/:orderRef")
- After placing order, customer is redirected here
- 6-digit OTP input with auto-focus between digits
- OTP is sent to customer's email
- "Resend OTP" button with 60-second cooldown timer
- On successful verification, redirect to tracking page

PAGE 5 — ORDER TRACKING ("/track/:orderRef")
- Visual status timeline showing order progress:
  Verified → Accepted → Paid → Shipped → Delivered
- Active step highlighted, completed steps checked
- When status is "accepted": show "Pay Now" button that opens Razorpay payment link
- When status is "shipped": show AWB number, courier name, tracking URL
- Order items breakdown with total
- Confetti animation when payment is confirmed (URL callback with ?payment=success)

=== CART SYSTEM ===
- Use Zustand for state management with localStorage persistence
- Cart persists across page refreshes and navigation
- Cart state: array of { productId, name, price, emoji, quantity }
- Actions: addToCart, removeFromCart, updateQuantity, clearCart
- Derived: totalItems, subtotal

=== DESIGN REQUIREMENTS ===
- Dark theme with glassmorphism cards (semi-transparent backgrounds, blur, subtle borders)
- Background: #06060B (near black)
- Accent color: #7C6FE9 (purple) for buttons and highlights
- Secondary accent: #34D399 (green) for success states
- Font: Google Fonts — "Manrope" for body, "Fira Code" for monospace/prices
- Mobile-first responsive design — 768px breakpoint
- Smooth CSS animations: cards fade-up on load, buttons scale on hover, page transitions
- All cards use: background with rgba transparency, backdrop-filter blur, 1px border with low opacity white

=== TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS ===
- React + TypeScript + Vite
- React Router v6 for page navigation with lazy-loaded routes
- Zustand for cart state management
- Custom i18n system — all UI text in a translations object supporting Hindi and English, with a toggle
- All API calls go to a configurable VITE_API_URL environment variable (e.g., "https://yourstore.com/api")
- Mobile responsive — 2-column product grid on mobile, sidebar on desktop
- All product data fetched from API (GET /api/catalog), not hardcoded
- Proper loading states and error handling on all pages

=== API ENDPOINTS THE FRONTEND SHOULD CALL ===
GET /api/catalog — returns product list with categories
GET /api/catalog/:id — returns single product + related products
POST /api/order — place order (body: { items, customer_name, customer_email, customer_phone, delivery_type, notes })
POST /api/order/verify — verify OTP (body: { order_ref, otp })
POST /api/order/resend-otp — resend OTP (body: { order_ref })
GET /api/track/:order_ref — get order status and details

=== OUTPUT ===
Give me the complete file structure and ALL files with full code. Every file should be complete — no placeholders, no "// add more here", no truncation. Include:
- package.json with all dependencies
- vite.config.ts
- All components, pages, stores, utils, styles
- Instructions to run: npm install && npm run dev
prompts.tip: Yeh prompt tumhara poora frontend bana dega — products, cart, checkout, OTP, tracking, payments sab. Brackets [ ] mein apne store ke details replace karo.

prompts.label: ⚡ Prompt 2 — Copy & Paste into Claude
prompts.title: Cloudflare Workers Backend with D1 Database, OTP, Razorpay & Email
prompts.text:
Build me a complete backend API using Cloudflare Workers with D1 (SQLite) database and KV storage. This powers an ecommerce store for Indian small businesses — handling orders, payments, OTP verification, stock management, and transactional emails.

=== CONFIGURATION (REPLACE WITH YOUR OWN) ===
- Admin Email: [your email — e.g., "owner@yourstore.com"]
- Admin API Key: [a secret key you create — e.g., "sk-your-secret-key-here"]
- Store Name: [Your Store Name]
- Store Domain: [e.g., "yourstore.com"]
- Razorpay Key ID: [from Razorpay dashboard]
- Razorpay Key Secret: [from Razorpay dashboard]
- Resend API Key: [from resend.com — free tier, 100 emails/day]

=== CLOUDFLARE BINDINGS ===
- D1 Database binding: env.DB
- KV Namespace binding: env.STORE_KV (used for OTP storage, rate limiting, admin sessions)
- All secrets stored via "wrangler secret put": RESEND_API_KEY, RAZORPAY_KEY_ID, RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET, RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAIL, ADMIN_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

=== DATABASE SCHEMA (D1 / SQLite) ===

TABLE: products
- id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
- name TEXT NOT NULL
- sku TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
- description TEXT
- category TEXT NOT NULL
- unit TEXT DEFAULT 'pcs' (options: pcs, kg, g, l, ml, pack)
- price REAL NOT NULL
- cost_price REAL
- stock_qty INTEGER DEFAULT 0
- min_qty INTEGER DEFAULT 5 (threshold for low stock alert)
- max_daily_qty INTEGER DEFAULT 10 (max a single order can have of this item)
- is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1
- image_url TEXT
- supplier TEXT
- supplier_email TEXT
- created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
- updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))

TABLE: orders
- id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
- order_ref TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL (format: "ORD-XXXXX" random 5 char alphanumeric)
- customer_name TEXT NOT NULL
- customer_email TEXT NOT NULL
- customer_phone TEXT
- status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' (values: pending, verified, accepted, paid, shipped, delivered, rejected, expired)
- otp_verified INTEGER DEFAULT 0
- total_amount REAL NOT NULL
- delivery_type TEXT DEFAULT 'delivery' (values: delivery, pickup)
- delivery_charge REAL DEFAULT 0
- payment_link_id TEXT
- payment_link_url TEXT
- payment_status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending'
- payment_id TEXT
- payment_expires_at TEXT
- ai_decision TEXT (values: approved, flagged)
- ai_reasoning TEXT
- flag_reasons TEXT
- awb_number TEXT
- shipping_provider TEXT
- tracking_url TEXT
- shipped_at TEXT
- delivered_at TEXT
- paid_at TEXT
- notes TEXT
- created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
- updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))

TABLE: order_items
- id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
- order_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id)
- product_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id)
- product_name TEXT NOT NULL
- quantity INTEGER NOT NULL
- unit_price REAL NOT NULL
- total_price REAL NOT NULL

TABLE: purchase_orders
- id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
- po_ref TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
- supplier TEXT NOT NULL
- supplier_email TEXT
- status TEXT DEFAULT 'draft' (values: draft, sent, received)
- items_json TEXT NOT NULL (JSON string of items array)
- total_estimate REAL
- ai_reasoning TEXT
- approved_at TEXT
- sent_at TEXT
- created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))

TABLE: daily_summaries
- id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
- summary_date TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
- total_orders INTEGER DEFAULT 0
- total_revenue REAL DEFAULT 0
- total_paid INTEGER DEFAULT 0
- top_products TEXT (JSON string)
- low_stock TEXT (JSON string)
- ai_insights TEXT
- created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))

=== PUBLIC API ROUTES (no auth required) ===

GET /api/catalog
- Returns all active products grouped by category
- Response: { products: [...], categories: ["Grocery", "Dairy", ...] }

GET /api/catalog/:id
- Returns single product details + 4 related products (same category)
- Response: { product: {...}, related: [...] }

POST /api/order
- Body: { items: [{product_id, quantity}], customer_name, customer_email, customer_phone, delivery_type, notes }
- Validates: all items exist and are in stock, quantities within max_daily_qty
- Calculates total: sum of (unit_price * quantity) + delivery_charge (₹40 if delivery, 0 if pickup)
- Generates unique order_ref (ORD-XXXXX)
- Generates 6-digit OTP, stores in KV with 10-minute TTL (key: "otp:{order_ref}")
- Saves order to DB with status "pending"
- Sends OTP email to customer using Resend
- Response: { order_ref, message: "OTP sent to your email" }

POST /api/order/verify
- Body: { order_ref, otp }
- Validates OTP from KV store
- On success: updates order status to "verified", deletes OTP from KV
- Rate limit: max 5 attempts per order_ref (tracked in KV)
- Response: { success: true, redirect: "/track/{order_ref}" }

POST /api/order/resend-otp
- Body: { order_ref }
- Rate limit: max 3 resends, 60-second cooldown between resends (KV-tracked)
- Generates new OTP, stores in KV, sends email
- Response: { success: true, message: "New OTP sent" }

GET /api/track/:order_ref
- Returns order status, items, payment info, shipping info
- Response: { order: {...}, items: [...] }

POST /api/payment/webhook
- Razorpay webhook handler (event: "payment_link.paid")
- Verifies webhook signature using RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET
- Updates order: payment_status = "paid", status = "paid", paid_at = now
- Sends payment confirmation email to customer

=== ADMIN API ROUTES (auth required — check Authorization header against ADMIN_API_KEY) ===

POST /api/auth/login
- Body: { email, api_key }
- Validates email matches ADMIN_EMAIL and api_key matches ADMIN_API_KEY
- Generates OTP, stores in KV, sends to admin email
- Response: { message: "OTP sent" }

POST /api/auth/verify
- Body: { email, otp }
- Validates OTP, creates session token in KV (24h TTL)
- Response: { token: "session-xxxxx" }

GET /api/orders
- Query params: ?status=&page=&limit=15
- Returns paginated orders, newest first, flagged orders always on top
- Each order includes: order_ref, customer_name, status, total_amount, delivery_type, ai_decision, created_at

GET /api/order/:id
- Returns full order detail with items

PATCH /api/order/:id/accept
- Changes status to "accepted"
- Creates Razorpay payment link via Razorpay API (amount = total_amount, expire_by = 1 hour from now)
- Saves payment_link_id and payment_link_url to order
- Sends payment link email to customer
- Response: { payment_link_url }

PATCH /api/order/:id/reject
- Body: { reason }
- Changes status to "rejected"
- Sends rejection email to customer with reason

PATCH /api/order/:id/ship
- Body: { awb_number, shipping_provider, tracking_url }
- Changes status to "shipped", saves shipping details, sets shipped_at
- Sends shipping confirmation email to customer

PATCH /api/order/:id/deliver
- Changes status to "delivered", sets delivered_at
- Works for both: shipped orders (delivery) AND paid+pickup orders (marks directly as delivered)

GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/stock/:id
- Full CRUD for products
- GET: list all products with stock levels
- POST: add new product
- PATCH: update product details/stock
- DELETE: soft delete (set is_active = 0)

=== RATE LIMITING ===
- KV-based rate limiting per route per IP
- POST /api/order: 10 requests per minute
- POST /api/order/verify: 5 requests per minute
- Admin routes skip rate limiting (check Authorization header)

=== EMAIL SYSTEM (using Resend API) ===
All emails sent from "Store Name <orders@yourdomain.com>" via Resend.
Include delivery type badge (🚚 Delivery / 🏪 Pickup) and delivery notes in all emails.

6 transactional email templates:
1. OTP Email — verification code + order summary
2. Payment Link Email — order accepted, "Pay Now" button, 1-hour expiry warning
3. Payment Confirmed — receipt with items + tracking link
4. Shipped Email — AWB number, courier name, tracking link
5. Rejected Email — reason + "Place New Order" link
6. Expired Email — payment window missed + "Reorder" link

=== RAZORPAY INTEGRATION ===
- Use Razorpay Payment Links API (not checkout.js — this is backend-only)
- Create payment link: POST https://api.razorpay.com/v1/payment_links
- Auth: Basic auth with key_id:key_secret
- Payment link body: { amount (in paise), currency: "INR", description, customer: { name, email, contact }, expire_by (unix timestamp), callback_url, callback_method: "get" }
- Webhook: verify X-Razorpay-Signature header using HMAC SHA256

=== OUTPUT ===
Give me the complete Cloudflare Worker project with ALL files:
- wrangler.toml with D1 and KV bindings
- seed.sql with 15-20 sample products across all categories
- schema.sql with all table definitions
- src/index.ts — main worker with router
- All route handlers, middleware, email templates, utils
- Every file complete — no placeholders, no truncation
- Deploy command: wrangler deploy
prompts.tip: Yeh prompt tumhara poora backend bana dega — database, orders, OTP, payments, emails sab. Cloudflare Workers pe free mein deploy hoga. Brackets [ ] mein apne details daalo.

prompts.label: 🧠 Prompt 3 — Copy & Paste into Claude
prompts.title: AI Fraud Detection Agent + Stock Analysis Agent + Daily Cron Job
prompts.text:
Add two AI agents and a daily cron job to my existing Cloudflare Workers ecommerce backend. The backend already has: D1 database (orders, order_items, products, purchase_orders, daily_summaries tables), KV storage, Resend for emails, and Razorpay for payments. I need AI-powered order validation, stock intelligence, and automated daily analysis.

=== CONFIGURATION ===
- AI Provider: Anthropic Claude API (model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514)
- API Key: stored as secret ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- All AI responses should be in Hindi/Hinglish (the store owner speaks Hindi)
- Existing bindings: env.DB (D1), env.STORE_KV (KV), env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

=== AI AGENT 1: ORDER FRAUD DETECTION ===

This agent runs automatically every time a new order is verified (after OTP verification succeeds). It analyzes the order and decides: approve or flag.

TRIGGER: Call this agent inside the POST /api/order/verify handler, right after OTP verification succeeds.

INPUT TO AI: Send the following data to Claude:
1. The new order: customer_name, customer_email, customer_phone, items (with quantities and prices), total_amount, delivery_type, notes
2. Last 24 hours of orders from same email (query DB: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_email = ? AND created_at > datetime('now', '-1 day'))
3. Current stock levels for all items in the order (query DB: SELECT stock_qty, min_qty, max_daily_qty FROM products WHERE id IN (...))

SYSTEM PROMPT FOR AGENT 1:
"You are a fraud detection AI for an Indian kiryana store. Analyze each order and decide: APPROVE or FLAG.

Check for these red flags:
- Same email placing multiple orders in 24 hours
- Unusually high quantities (above max_daily_qty for any item)
- Total amount above ₹5000 (unusual for a kiryana store)
- Delivery notes that seem suspicious or test-like ("test", "asdf", "xyz")
- Bulk ordering of a single item (e.g., 20 units of one product)

Respond in this exact JSON format:
{
  \"decision\": \"approved\" or \"flagged\",
  \"reasoning\": \"Hindi mein explain karo kyu approve ya flag kiya\",
  \"flag_reasons\": [\"list of specific red flags found\"] or []
}

Be helpful to the shopkeeper — explain in simple Hindi why you made the decision. If everything looks normal, approve it. Only flag genuinely suspicious orders."

AFTER AI RESPONSE:
- Parse the JSON response
- Update the order in DB: SET ai_decision = decision, ai_reasoning = reasoning, flag_reasons = JSON.stringify(flag_reasons), status = 'verified'
- If decision is "approved": you can optionally auto-change status to "accepted" (configurable)
- If decision is "flagged": keep status as "verified" so admin manually reviews it

=== AI AGENT 2: STOCK ANALYSIS + PURCHASE ORDER GENERATION ===

This agent analyzes today's orders and current stock levels, generates business insights, identifies low stock items with urgency levels, and auto-creates purchase orders for critical items.

TRIGGER: Called via admin API route POST /api/agent/analyze (admin clicks "Run AI" button) and also via daily cron job.

INPUT TO AI: Send the following data to Claude:
1. Today's orders: all orders from today with items and statuses (query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE DATE(created_at) = DATE('now'))
2. Today's order items with product details (JOIN order_items with products)
3. Full product stock list: all products with stock_qty, min_qty, cost_price, supplier, supplier_email
4. Previous day's summary (if exists): from daily_summaries table

SYSTEM PROMPT FOR AGENT 2:
"You are a business intelligence AI for an Indian kiryana store. Analyze today's orders and stock levels. Respond in Hindi/Hinglish.

Your job:
1. DAILY SUMMARY: Provide a brief business summary — total orders, revenue, any notable patterns, comparison with yesterday if data available.

2. LOW STOCK ALERTS: Identify products where stock_qty is below or near min_qty. For each:
   - Product name and current stock
   - Urgency level: CRITICAL (stock = 0), HIGH (stock < min_qty), MEDIUM (stock < min_qty * 1.5)
   - Specific recommendation in Hindi (e.g., 'Aaj yeh bik gayi hai, turant restock karo')

3. PURCHASE ORDERS: For CRITICAL and HIGH urgency items, generate purchase orders grouped by supplier. Each PO should include:
   - Supplier name and email
   - Items to order with recommended quantities (typically min_qty * 2 to avoid frequent reordering)
   - Estimated cost (quantity * cost_price)
   - Hindi reasoning for why this PO is needed

4. INSIGHTS: Any interesting patterns — which products are selling fast, which categories are popular, any unusual activity.

Respond in this exact JSON format:
{
  \"summary\": \"Hindi mein aaj ka business summary\",
  \"stats\": { \"total_orders\": N, \"total_revenue\": N, \"paid_orders\": N, \"flagged_count\": N },
  \"low_stock\": [
    {
      \"product_id\": N,
      \"product_name\": \"...\",
      \"stock_qty\": N,
      \"min_qty\": N,
      \"urgency\": \"critical|high|medium\",
      \"recommendation\": \"Hindi mein recommendation\"
    }
  ],
  \"purchase_orders\": [
    {
      \"supplier\": \"Supplier Name\",
      \"supplier_email\": \"email@supplier.com\",
      \"items\": [
        { \"product_id\": N, \"product_name\": \"...\", \"quantity\": N, \"unit\": \"pcs\", \"estimated_cost\": N }
      ],
      \"total_estimate\": N,
      \"reasoning\": \"Hindi mein explain karo kyu yeh PO generate kiya\"
    }
  ],
  \"insights\": \"Hindi mein interesting patterns and observations\"
}"

AFTER AI RESPONSE:
- Parse the JSON response
- Save to daily_summaries table: summary_date, total_orders, total_revenue, total_paid, top_products (from insights), low_stock (JSON), ai_insights
- For each purchase_order in response: INSERT into purchase_orders table with status "draft"
- Return the full analysis to the admin dashboard

=== ADMIN API ROUTES FOR AI ===

POST /api/agent/analyze
- Auth required
- Gathers all data, calls AI Agent 2, saves results
- Response: full analysis JSON

GET /api/agent/summary/:date
- Auth required
- Returns saved daily summary for the given date
- Response: { summary: {...}, low_stock: [...], purchase_orders: [...] }

GET /api/purchase-orders
- Auth required
- Query params: ?status=draft|sent|received
- Returns all purchase orders

PATCH /api/purchase-order/:id/approve
- Changes PO status from "draft" to "sent"
- Sends email to supplier with itemized PO using Resend
- Sets approved_at and sent_at timestamps

PATCH /api/purchase-order/:id/receive
- Changes PO status from "sent" to "received"
- Optionally: auto-update product stock_qty (add received quantities)

=== CRON JOB (Cloudflare Workers Cron Trigger) ===

Set up a Cloudflare Workers cron trigger that runs daily at 11:00 PM IST (17:30 UTC).

In wrangler.toml add:
[triggers]
crons = ["30 17 * * *"]

The cron handler should:
1. Run AI Agent 2 (same logic as POST /api/agent/analyze)
2. Save daily summary to database
3. If any CRITICAL stock items found: send alert email to admin with the low stock items and generated POs
4. Log completion in KV: "cron:last_run" = current timestamp

=== IMPORTANT NOTES ===
- All AI calls use the Anthropic Messages API: POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages
- Headers: x-api-key, anthropic-version: "2023-06-01", content-type: application/json
- Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514, max_tokens: 4096
- Parse AI response carefully — strip markdown code fences if present before JSON.parse()
- Handle AI errors gracefully — if AI call fails, log error but don't break the order flow
- The fraud detection agent should NOT block order verification — if AI fails, default to "approved" and continue

=== ZERO COST ALTERNATIVE ===
If you want completely ₹0 AI cost, replace the Anthropic API with Cloudflare Workers AI:
- Model: @cf/meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct
- Binding: env.AI (add [ai] section in wrangler.toml)
- Call: const response = await env.AI.run("@cf/meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct", { messages: [...] })
- This is 100% free on Cloudflare Workers. Slightly less accurate than Claude but perfectly fine for a small store.

=== OUTPUT ===
Give me ALL the new and modified files:
- Updated wrangler.toml with cron trigger
- AI agent modules (agent-fraud.ts, agent-stock.ts)
- New API route handlers for AI endpoints
- Updated order verification handler to call fraud agent
- Cron handler (scheduled event)
- Email template for supplier PO and admin stock alerts
- Every file complete — no placeholders, no truncation
- Clear comments explaining what each section does
prompts.tip: Yeh prompt tumhare store mein AI brain daal dega. Fraud detection automatic, stock alerts automatic, daily cron job automatic. Ek baar set karo, daily chalega — tumhe kuch karna nahi hai.
